lede: A look at January 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jacob Grimm and Emperor Zhezong, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Koszalin escape room fire
events.desc: A fire in an escape room in Koszalin, Poland, kills five teenagers through carbon monoxide poisoning.

events.title: Burj Khalifa
events.desc: The Burj Khalifa, the current tallest building in the world, officially opens in Dubai.

events.title: Spirit (rover)
events.desc: Spirit, a NASA Mars rover, lands successfully on Mars at 04:35 UTC.

events.title: Rose Heilbron
events.desc: Rose Heilbron becomes the first female judge to sit at the Old Bailey in London, UK.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: Chinese and North Korean forces capture Seoul for the second time.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Carpetbagger, involving the dropping of arms and supplies to resistance fighters in Europe, begins.

events.title: Aeneas Mackintosh
events.desc: Explorer Aeneas Mackintosh of the Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition escaped death by fleeing across ice floes.

events.title: Utah
events.desc: Utah is admitted as the 45th U.S. state.

events.title: Solomon Northup
events.desc: After having been kidnapped and sold into slavery in the American South, Solomon Northup regains his freedom; his memoir Twelve Years a Slave later becomes a national bestseller.

events.title: Spain
events.desc: Great Britain declares war on Spain, which meant the entry of Spain into the Seven Years' War.

births.name: Gao Xingjian
births.line: Chinese novelist, playwright, and critic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Georg Elser
births.line: German carpenter and attempted assassin of Adolf Hitler

deaths.text: T. S. Eliot, American-English poet, playwright, and critic, Nobel Prize laureate
Erwin Schrödinger, Austrian physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Albert Camus, French novelist, philosopher, and journalist, Nobel Prize laureate
Henri Bergson, French philosopher and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: The eleventh of the Twelve Days of Christmas. (Western Christianity)
